title: Denarii Nova
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Star |image=[[File:DenariiNova-FotSE-1.jpg]] |name=Denarii Nova |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *[[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] |sector=[[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]] |system=*[[Denarii system]] {{C|formerly}} *[[Denarii Nebula/Legends|Denarii Nebula]]''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]''''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |coordinates=P-6 |xyz= |routes= |distance= |orbits= |stations=[[Shadow Academy]] |asteroids= |other= |class= |diameter= }} {{Quote|Set course for the Denarii Nova! I have a plan to make use of the remaining power we have!|Naga Sadow|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]]}} The '''Denarii Nova''' was the name given to a [[Binary star/Legends|pair]] of [[Star/Legends|stars]] located in the [[Denarii system]], and later in the [[Denarii Nebula/Legends|Denarii Nebula]], of the [[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]] [[Sector/Legends|sector]], within the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was known to be far removed from the [[Black Hole of Quintas/Legends|Black Hole of Quintas]].''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'' The smaller star of the Denarii Nova fed on [[Gas/Legends|gases]] stolen from the larger one.[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]] ==History== {{Quote|The Force is in all things, from the smallest pebble to the largest star. This is just a glimmer of how Naga Sadow reached out to the stars and delivered a mortal wound five thousand years ago.''"
"''Could you make the sun explode?''"
"''I don't know. And I don't believe I ever want to try.|[[Brakiss]] and [[Zekk]]|}} [[File:Denarii_supernova.png|thumb|left|220px|The explosion created by Naga Sadow]] At the end of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 4]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] flew his damaged [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Corsair (Derriphan-class)|''Corsair'']], between the [[Binary star/Legends|binary stars]] of the Denarii Nova to escape the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Koros fleet|forces]] that pursued him. The [[Sith sorcerer]] [[Battle at the Denarii Nova|used]] his [[Sith sorcery/Legends|powers]] to [[Supernova (Sith magic)|manipulate]] the [[Solar flare/Legends|solar flares]] of the Denarii Nova, thus creating an explosion that destroyed the Republic [[Starship/Legends|ships]] in his wake. This cataclysmic event resulted in the destruction of the entire [[Denarii system]], which created a [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] of highly ionized [[Gas/Legends|gases]] known as the [[Denarii Nebula/Legends|Denarii Nebula]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], eight [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the fanatical [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Ishin-Il-Raz]] committed suicide by plunging his [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], the ''[[Emperor's Disciple]]'', into the [[Fire/Legends|firestorm]] of the Denarii Nova, killing himself and his entire crew.{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} During the [[Second Imperium]] [[Second Imperium Crisis|crisis]] in [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], following a [[Raid on Coruscant (Second Imperium Crisis)|raid]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]],''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Lost Ones]]'' the [[Shadow Academy]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] found a new hiding place near the dying [[Star/Legends|stars]] of the Denarii Nova, within the Denarii Nebula. {{Star-stub}} ==Appearances==
